task_completion.description: Open the initial rocket design in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is visible with the 'zipper_prevention.ork' file loaded.

task_completion.description: Edit the motor configurations to set new integer delays for the Estes E16, AeroTech F67, and AeroTech G40 motors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Motor Configuration dialog is open, showing the delay fields modified to new integer values (different from the original 15s, 14s, and 0s).

task_completion.description: Run simulations to verify that the deployment velocity is strictly less than 15 m/s for all configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Simulations tab is visible showing completed simulations, with the 'Deployment velocity' column displaying values strictly less than 15 m/s.

task_completion.description: Ensure the parachute deployment event remains set to 'Motor ejection charge'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Parachute configuration dialog or simulation settings show the deployment trigger explicitly set to 'Motor ejection charge' (and NOT 'Apogee').

task_completion.description: Save the corrected design to the specified new file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/zipper_prevention_fixed.ork' or the OpenRocket main window title updated to 'zipper_prevention_fixed.ork'.

task_completion.description: Write a flight preparation report listing each motor and its tuned delay.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/exports/delay_report.txt' containing a list of the three motors (E16, F67, G40) and their newly tuned integer delays.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket graphical user interface to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket GUI (menus, dialogs, simulation tabs) rather than editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the simulations to determine the optimal delays and velocities.
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulation results table in OpenRocket is populated with fresh flight data (apogee, velocity, etc.), indicating simulations were run.

integrity.description: The agent generated the report based on simulation results rather than hallucinating or copying the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor contains specific tuned integer delays that correspond to the modified OpenRocket configurations, rather than just repeating the original dangerous delays from the prompt.
